commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Then Naomi her mother-in-law said unto her, My daughter, shall I not seek rest for you, that it may be well with you. Hebrew and Septuagint may be read with an interrogation in the same sense. "Shall I not seek rest. "(Haydock) By this expression she means a husband, chap. Marriage fixes the unsettled condition of women. (Calmet) Noemi being apprised of the law, entertained hopes that she could engage Booz to marry Ruth. (Haydock) Thus her penury would cease, and she would perhaps have children, as she earnestly desired. (Menochius).
